source_article.title_en: Fantasy Baseball Week 7 Preview: Top 10 sleeper hitters include Moises Ballesteros, Nathaniel Lowe
source_article.author: Scott White
source_article.dek_en: Sleeper hitters for Fantasy Week 7, waiver wire targets, and breakout candidates
source_article.body_en:
Whether you're in need of an injury fill-in or just a hot-hand play, Scott White has you covered with 10 sleeper hitters for the upcoming scoring period, all rostered in less than 80 percent of CBS Sports leagues. They're not must-starts by any estimation, but they're often the best you'll find off the waiver wire.

All information is up to date as of Sunday evening.

1. Marlins PHI1, BAL3, WAS3 2. Phillies @MIA1, ATH3, COL3 3. Mets @COL3, @ARI3 4. Cardinals MIL3, @SD4 5. Giants SD3, PIT3

1. Astros LAD3, @CIN3 2. Braves @SEA3, @LAD3 3. Guardians @KC4, MIN3 4. Diamondbacks PIT3, NYM3 5. Yankees BAL1, TEX3, @MIL3

1. Moises Ballesteros (C) - Moises Ballesteros earned high marks as a hitter in the minors and has begun to live up to it in the majors, too, carrying a .317/.380/.598 slash line into the new week. He still tends to sit against left-handers, but the Cubs are in line to face just one of those in a seven-game scoring period.
2. Carter Jensen (C) - Left-handers can also sometimes pose a problem for Carter Jensen, but the Royals are scheduled to face just two in their seven games this week. You may have a hard time fitting in another catcher in an environment rich with them, but Jensen is good enough and plays often enough to suffice in a utility spot as well.
3. Spencer Torkelson (1B) - After a stretch of five straight games with a home run toward the end of April, Spencer Torkelson just added a sixth Sunday, further diminishing concerns that he won't be able to live up to last year's 31 homers. Four of the pitchers on tap for the Tigers this week throw left-handed, which should work to the benefit of a guy whose batting average was 30 points higher and OPS was 130 points higher against lefties than righties last year.
4. Nate Lowe (1B) - Nate Lowe has served as the Reds' everyday DH since Eugenio Suarez was lost to an oblique injury and has been obliterating the ball in that role, homering five times with an average exit velocity of 92.3 mph. His strikeout and pull-air rates are the best they've ever been, and he's looking at a slate of hittable righties like Jameson Taillon, Colin Rea, Michael Burrows and Kai-Wei Teng.
5. Brandon Marsh (CF) - Brandon Marsh is looking like a more complete hitter this year, bringing his strikeout rate down to a respectable level and getting occasional chances against left-handed pitchers. Even if he ends up sitting against the two on the schedule this week, his power/speed profile makes him the Phillies hitter most poised to take advantage of their second-ranked hitter matchups.
6. Cole Young (SS) - Cole Young's massive spring hasn't translated to the regular season, but he is finally beginning to make an impact, batting .367 (11 for 30) with two doubles, a home run and a stolen base in his past eight games. He has some attractive matchups this week against the back of the Braves rotation and the White Sox pitching staff.
7. Spencer Steer (1B) - Spencer Steer has been barreling up the ball consistently, with a 17.6 percent rate that ranks in the 93rd percentile, and it's begun to bear fruit with him batting .290 (18 for 62) with four homers and three doubles in his past 20 games. As already discussed for Nate Lowe, the Reds' matchups are pretty favorable this week.
8. Nathan Church (OF) - Nathan Church's average exit velocity has been creeping up over the past couple weeks, combining with his high pull-air rate for a surprising home run output. He tends to sit against left-handers, but the Cardinals aren't scheduled to face any of those this week, which makes Church a prime candidate to take advantage of the fourth-best hitter schedule.
9. Dominic Canzone (LF) - Dominic Canzone's Baseball Savant page continues to burn as red as Betelgeuse, with his 95.7 mph average exit velocity standing out in particular. His biggest drawback is that he never ever plays against left-handed pitchers, but the Mariners have only one of those scheduled for this week.
10. Jung Hoo Lee (CF) - Improved contact quality and a higher launch angle has led to a .281 batting average and even better .291 xBA for Jung Hoo Lee. The Giants' fifth-ranked hitter matchups are all the more appealing to him given that all the opposing pitchers are right-handers. He has a batting average 40 points higher and an OPS 140 points higher against righties than lefties in his career.

He still tends to sit against left-handers, but the Cubs are in line to face just one of those in a seven-game scoring period.

The Giants' fifth-ranked hitter matchups are all the more appealing to him given that all the opposing pitchers are right-handers. He has a batting average 40 points higher and an OPS 140 points higher against righties than lefties in his career.

ai_article.article_summary_zh_tw: CBS Sports 這篇第 7 週打者睡眠名單整理了 10 位可從免錢名單補強的打者，重點放在近期手感、對左右投拆分與本週賽程優勢。

players.summary_en: Carries a .317/.380/.598 slash line and should benefit from facing just one left-hander in a seven-game week.
players.summary_zh_tw: 他目前帶著 .317/.380/.598 的打擊三圍進入本週，而且小熊七場比賽只會遇到一名左投，對他相當有利。

players.summary_en: Has enough playing time and will face only two left-handers in seven games, making him viable even in a utility spot.
players.summary_zh_tw: 他本週七場只會碰上兩名左投，加上出賽量足夠，因此就算放在 utility 也算實用。

players.summary_en: Has homered in six straight games and gets a favorable slate with four left-handed pitchers on tap.
players.summary_zh_tw: 他已經連續六場開轟，而老虎本週還要面對四名左投，情勢對他很有利。

players.summary_en: Has been crushing the ball as the Reds' everyday DH with five homers and a strong schedule of hittable right-handers ahead.
players.summary_zh_tw: 他在紅人隊固定擔任指定打擊後火力全開，已敲出五轟，而且本週對手多是好打的右投。

players.summary_en: Is showing a more complete bat and offers power-speed upside despite possible sit days against lefties.
players.summary_zh_tw: 他今年展現更完整的打擊內容，雖然可能會輪休對左投的比賽，但仍有長打與速度的雙重潛力。

players.summary_en: Has started producing in the majors with a .367 average, two doubles, a homer and a steal over his last eight games.
players.summary_zh_tw: 他最近八場打出 .367 打擊率，並貢獻二壘打、全壘打與盜壘，終於開始在大聯盟發揮。

players.summary_en: Is barreling the ball at an elite rate and has hit .290 with four homers over his last 20 games.
players.summary_zh_tw: 他近期的強勁擊球率相當出色，過去 20 場又繳出 .290 打擊率和四轟。

players.summary_en: Has seen his exit velocity rise and could take advantage of a Cardinals schedule with no left-handers.
players.summary_zh_tw: 他最近的平均擊球初速持續上升，而紅雀本週沒有左投可遇，正好放大他的價值。

players.summary_en: Owns loud exit velocity numbers and should get more chances because the Mariners have only one left-hander scheduled.
players.summary_zh_tw: 他擁有很亮眼的擊球初速數據，而且水手本週只排到一名左投，讓他的上場空間更好。

players.summary_en: Has improved contact quality and a favorable split against right-handers, who make up all of the Giants' opposing pitchers this week.
players.summary_zh_tw: 他本季擊球品質與仰角都有提升，而巨人本週面對的投手全是右投，對他格外有利。

players.summary_en: Carries a .317/.380/.598 slash line and should benefit from facing just one left-hander in a seven-game week.
